SEARCH
Package com.unimib.assignment3.service

Class EmployeeService

java.lang.Object
com.unimib.assignment3.service.EmployeeService
Direct Known Subclasses:
SupervisorService
@Service public class EmployeeService extends Object
Service class for managing Dipendente (employee) entities.

Provides CRUD operations, salary and role updates, task queries, and manager-authorized actions.

  • Constructor Details

    • EmployeeService

      public EmployeeService()

  • Method Details

    • saveEmployee

      public Employee saveEmployee(@NonNull Employee employee)
      Save a single employee to the database and handles email uniqueness.
      Parameters:
      employee - the employee to save
      Returns:
      the saved employee
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if email is not unique and if employee is null

    • saveAllEmployees

      public List<Employee> saveAllEmployees(@NonNull List<Employee> employees)
      Save multiple employees to the database in bulk.
      Parameters:
      employees - list of employees
      Returns:
      list of saved employees
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the employees list is null

    • checkUniqueEmail

      protected void checkUniqueEmail(Employee employee)
      Ensure the employee's email is unique by appending a counter if necessary.
      Parameters:
      employee - the employee whose email needs to be checked
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the employee is null

    • createEmployee

      public Employee createEmployee(@NonNull String name, @NonNull String surname)
      Create a new employee with the given name and surname.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the employee (must not be null)
      surname - the surname of the employee (must not be null)
      Returns:
      the created supervisor entity
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the name or surname is null

    • createEmployee

      public Employee createEmployee(@NonNull String name, @NonNull String surname, double monthlySalary, @NonNull EmployeeRole employeeRole)
      Create a new employee with the given name, surname, monthly salary, and employee role.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the supervisor (must not be null)
      surname - the surname of the supervisor (must not be null)
      monthlySalary - the monthly salary of the supervisor
      employeeRole - the role of the supervisor (must not be null)
      Returns:
      the created supervisor entity
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the name, surname, or employee role is null or if the email is not unique

    • checkSalary

      protected void checkSalary(double monthlySalary, EmployeeRole employeeRole)

    • findEmployeeById

      public Optional<Employee> findEmployeeById(@NonNull Long employeeId)
      Find an employee by their ID.
      Parameters:
      employeeId - the ID of the employee
      Returns:
      Optional containing the employee if found
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the employeeId is null and the employee does not exist

    • findAllEmployees

      public List<Employee> findAllEmployees()
      Retrieve all employees.
      Returns:
      list of all employees

    • deleteEmployeeById

      public void deleteEmployeeById(@NonNull Long employeeId)
      Delete an employee by ID.
      Parameters:
      employeeId - the ID of the employee to delete
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the employeeId is null

    • fireEmployee

      public void fireEmployee(@NonNull Long managerId, @NonNull Long employeeId)
      Fire (delete) a single employee. Only a manager can perform this action.
      Parameters:
      managerId - the manager's ID
      employeeId - the employee's ID to fire
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the ids are null, if the manager is not authorized and if the employee does not exist

    • fireEmployees

      public void fireEmployees(@NonNull Long managerId, @NonNull List<Employee> employees)
      Fire multiple employees at once. Only a manager can perform this action.
      Parameters:
      managerId - the manager's ID
      employees - list of employees to fire
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the ids are null, if the manager is not authorized and if any of the employees do not exist or the employees list is null

    • findEmployeesByMonthlySalary

      public List<Employee> findEmployeesByMonthlySalary(@NonNull Long managerId, double monthlySalary)
      Find employees with a specific monthly salary. Only accessible by managers.
      Parameters:
      managerId - the manager's ID
      monthlySalary - the target salary
      Returns:
      list of employees with that salary
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the managerId is null and if the manager is not authorized and if the manager does not exist

    • findEmployeesByMonthlySalaryOrderByEmployeeRoleAsc

      public List<Employee> findEmployeesByMonthlySalaryOrderByEmployeeRoleAsc(@NonNull Long managerId, double monthlySalary)
      Find employees by salary and sort by role ascending. Only accessible by managers.
      Parameters:
      managerId - the manager's ID
      monthlySalary - the target salary
      Returns:
      list of employees with that salary ordered by role ascending
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the managerId is null and if the manager is not authorized and if the manager does not exist

    • findEmployeesByMonthlySalaryOrderByEmployeeRoleDesc

      public List<Employee> findEmployeesByMonthlySalaryOrderByEmployeeRoleDesc(@NonNull Long managerId, double monthlySalary)
      Find employees by salary and sort by role ascending. Only accessible by managers.
      Parameters:
      managerId - the manager's ID
      monthlySalary - the target salary
      Returns:
      list of employees with that salary ordered by role descending
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the managerId is null and if the manager is not authorized and if the manager does not exist

    • findEmployeesByEmployeeRole

      public List<Employee> findEmployeesByEmployeeRole(@NonNull Long managerId, @NonNull EmployeeRole employeeRole)
      Find employees by role. Only accessible by managers.
      Parameters:
      managerId - the manager's ID
      employeeRole - the target role
      Returns:
      list of employees with that role
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the managerId is null and if the manager is not authorized and if the manager does not exist, and if the employeeRole is null

    • findEmployeesByEmployeeRoleOrderByMonthlySalaryAsc

      public List<Employee> findEmployeesByEmployeeRoleOrderByMonthlySalaryAsc(@NonNull Long managerId, @NonNull EmployeeRole employeeRole)
      Find employees by role. Only accessible by managers.
      Parameters:
      managerId - the manager's ID
      employeeRole - the target role
      Returns:
      list of employees with that role ordered by salary ascending
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the managerId is null and if the manager is not authorized and if the manager does not exist, and if the employeeRole is null

    • findEmployeesByEmployeeRoleOrderByMonthlySalaryDesc

      public List<Employee> findEmployeesByEmployeeRoleOrderByMonthlySalaryDesc(@NonNull Long managerId, @NonNull EmployeeRole employeeRole)
      Find employees by role. Only accessible by managers.
      Parameters:
      managerId - the manager's ID
      employeeRole - the target role
      Returns:
      list of employees with that role ordered by salary descending
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the managerId is null and if the manager is not authorized and if the manager does not exist, and if the employeeRole is null

    • findTasksByEmployeeAndTaskState

      public List<Task> findTasksByEmployeeAndTaskState(@NonNull Long employeeId, @NonNull TaskState taskState)
      Retrieve all tasks assigned to an employee in a specific state.
      Parameters:
      employeeId - the employee's ID
      taskState - the state of tasks to filter
      Returns:
      list of tasks
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the employeeId or taskState is null

    • findTasksByEmployeeByTaskStateByStartDate

      public List<Task> findTasksByEmployeeByTaskStateByStartDate(@NonNull Long employeeId, @NonNull TaskState taskState, @NonNull LocalDate startDate)
      Retrieve all tasks assigned to an employee in a specific state, ordered by start date.
      Parameters:
      employeeId - the employee's ID
      taskState - the state of tasks to filter
      startDate - the start date to filter
      Returns:
      list of tasks
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the employeeId or taskState is null and if the startDate is null

    • findTasksByEmployeeByTaskStateByEndDate

      public List<Task> findTasksByEmployeeByTaskStateByEndDate(@NonNull Long employeeId, @NonNull TaskState taskState, @NonNull LocalDate endDate)
      Retrieve all tasks assigned to an employee in a specific state, ordered by end date.
      Parameters:
      employeeId - the employee's ID
      taskState - the state of tasks to filter
      endDate - the end date to filter
      Returns:
      list of tasks
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the employeeId or taskState is null and if the endDate is null

    • findTasksByEmployeeByTaskStateBetweenStartDateAndEndDate

      public List<Task> findTasksByEmployeeByTaskStateBetweenStartDateAndEndDate(@NonNull Long employeeId, @NonNull TaskState taskState, @NonNull LocalDate startDate, @NonNull LocalDate endDate)
      Retrieve all tasks assigned to an employee in a specific state, between start date and end date.
      Parameters:
      employeeId - the employee's ID
      taskState - the state of tasks to filter
      startDate - the start date to filter
      endDate - the end date to filter
      Returns:
      list of tasks
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the employeeId or taskState is null and if the startDate or endDate is null

    • findTasksByEmployeeByTaskStateOrderByStartDateDesc

      public List<Task> findTasksByEmployeeByTaskStateOrderByStartDateDesc(@NonNull Long employeeId, @NonNull TaskState taskState)
      Retrieve all tasks assigned to an employee in a specific state, ordered by start date descending.
      Parameters:
      employeeId - the employee's ID
      taskState - the state of tasks to filter
      Returns:
      list of tasks
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the employeeId or taskState is null and if the startDate is null

    • findTasksByEmployeeByTaskStateOrderByEndDateDesc

      public List<Task> findTasksByEmployeeByTaskStateOrderByEndDateDesc(@NonNull Long employeeId, @NonNull TaskState taskState)
      Retrieve all tasks assigned to an employee in a specific state, ordered by end date descending.
      Parameters:
      employeeId - the employee's ID
      taskState - the state of tasks to filter
      Returns:
      list of tasks
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the employeeId or taskState is null and if the endDate is null

    • updateMonthlySalaryById

      public void updateMonthlySalaryById(@NonNull Long managerId, @NonNull Long employeeId, double monthlySalary)
      Update an employee's monthly salary. Only a manager can perform this operation.
      Parameters:
      managerId - the manager's ID
      employeeId - the employee's ID
      monthlySalary - new monthly salary
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the ids are null, if the manager is not authorized and if the employee does not exist

    • updateEmployeeRoleById

      public void updateEmployeeRoleById(@NonNull Long managerId, @NonNull Long employeeId, @NonNull EmployeeRole employeeRole)
      Update an employee's role. Only a manager can perform this operation.
      Parameters:
      managerId - the manager's ID
      employeeId - the employee's ID
      employeeRole - the new role
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the ids are null, if the manager is not authorized, if the employee does not exist and if the employeeRole is null, and if the employeeRole is null

    • assertNotNull

      protected void assertNotNull(Object obj, String message) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Helper method to throw exception if the given object is null.
      Parameters:
      obj - given object to be checked
      message - to be thrown if the given object is null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the supervisor does not exist
